title: Talking about my life - Austin, Texas 3/21

transcript.text: Yeah, so, oops, yeah, because we had, there was a Rinpoche, we called him Rinpoche, Chagun
Trungpa Rinpoche, and one time he showed up in a helicopter, and I could feel all the
Like, wind come down, and when we were coming, when we would drive there from Austin, we
Would stop in Boulder, because Boulder had other Buddhist areas where we would go to
And get togethers, like Karmazong, we would go up, and so I knew Boulder from like, way
Back when I was in Austin, and I remember looking at the Flatirons and thinking that
They were really pretty, and I remember thinking that, because I knew that RMDC, Rocky Mountain
Dharma Center, this is called RMSC now, Rocky Mountain Shambhala Center, but RMDC was in
The mountains, and the mountains were behind the Flatirons, so I remember thinking that
The RMDC was behind the Flatirons, but it was farther north, it was behind the mountains
Outside of Fort Collins, not Boulder, but yeah, up there, I remember, yeah, they were
Like, they had lots of activities for kids, it was a really fun place, and there was sage
Everywhere, so whenever I smelled sage, even, maybe even to this day, it always made me remember
That place, and it brought good memories, and there were like these little houses where
They would dump the kids, and they'd have like, you know, activities for the kids, and
It was way out in the middle of nowhere, and we'd like, run around in the fields, it was
Like, really like, great place to like, become with nature, because we'd go on big hikes and
Stuff, one morning, we saw a dead mouse in the water, somebody had like, I guess, a water
Dishing thing open, it was floating in there and there, and like, there was another place
Where we were being babysat in some like, hut, and something, the dude who was babysitting
Us could walk on his hands, that was really cool, and there is this one like, really big
Hill next to RMDC, I want to go back there, I've been there since like, 84, and check
Out this hill, see how big it is, because there's this like, there's downtown area,
You kind of have this like, this main street, and there's this big flower garden in front
Of it, but like, just to the south of it, there was this like, big steep hill, and you'd
Hike up this hill, and there'd be like, rocks, and there was this like, little sandy, pristine
Little sandy area that you could sit there, and on the other side of it, there was this
A-frame, which people would do like, retreats in, and then there was like, really steep
Like, rock areas where you could climb up, and it was really cool, a lot of, a lot
Of adventuring around there, but yeah, but eventually, when I was back, I think it was
The summer of 80, when my brother was one, and my brother doesn't even remember Austin,
But I remember one time, we drove by the hospital, Austin Hospital, and they go, yeah, my mom's
Like, yeah, you were born there, I was like, yeah, I was born there, and my brother was
Like, yeah, me too, and he was like a little baby, so I guess, at least I think he said that,
I can't really remember, but, oh yeah, but I remember going into town, into Austin, because
We would like, drive by the capital, she'd be like, my mom'd be like, yeah, that's the
Capital, look, and we're like, oh cool, that's pretty, doing errands and stuff, and yeah,
And then, yeah, so then eventually, we moved to Boulder in the summer of 1980, and I remember
I didn't want to go to Boulder, I wanted to stay in Austin, but, and I remember when we
Left, they packed up, like, this big Mayflower shrug parked in front of the house, and they
Packed it up with, like, plants and stuff, and we had a whole bunch of stuff, and I guess
They destroyed a lot of our, like, furniture, and my parents were pissed off about that,
But we drove up, and we put the dog and the cat in the, in the back of the, uh, I don't
Know how we got the torn auto up, maybe somebody else drove it, because we were all in the,
Uh, Osmobile station wagon, and, uh, we had the dog and the cat in the back with us.
And we would do these games, like, oh, that's a mile, like, on the top of the hill, that's
A mile from here, and, like, we'd guess and get there and be like, oh yeah, that's a mile,
So I could calibrate exactly how far a mile was.
And there were these other things I did, like, when we were on road trips, like, Tyler, like,
I would, like, contemplate eternity, and just in my mind, I'd go off in the distance, and
Then off and off, and then, and then the idea of eternity, and time and space, and then
Truly tripped me out, and I liked tripping myself out over that.
And I did remember, I did believe in reincarnation from way back, at the beginning, because I
Remember one time we parked, uh, we were at, uh, like, a roadside kind of area after we
Had lunch or something, a dirt parking lot, and I don't know if this is the same episode,
But one time we did see, like, a pickup and a van, and my brother was like, I'm gonna get
A pickup when I'm a boulder, and I was like, and it was like this ruby-colored, maroon-colored,
Um, Ford van, and I was like, and I'm gonna get a van.
Vans are the coolest.
But I remember one time, like, we were out there, and I was like, I had sandals on that,
It was like leather sandals, and I, and I was on the dirt road, dirt parking lot, and I
Was like, yeah, man, like, Jesus and the disciples, I was like, maybe I was one of Jesus'
Disciples in a past life, because we, because it was kind of like, you know, like, I was
In the desert with the sandals on, and I kind of even had, like, the feeling of, like, being
Back there.
But I did have a dream once of a past life, vision.
And it was weird.
I opened up a magazine, and I saw, or maybe it was a dream, or maybe it was real, but
I could have sworn, I opened up a magazine, and I saw this person in a classroom pegged
To a wall, like, crucified to a wall on a door in a classroom, and then the person died.
And then that person, and I saw the picture of it in a magazine, I remember, I remember
Thinking, having, like, a past, like, memory, oh, that was me.
So then I was the person, and then the person died, and after the person died, the person,
You know, saw the light, went through the tunnel, and then was in this, like, office area.
I think this was a dream, but it was in an office area.
And by the way, if you start recalling dreams, you're going to recall, like, all of your dreams,
And you could talk for freaking hours about dreams you've had when you were five, dude.
It's, like, a part of your brain that you open up.
I have that on my dream journal.
But, like, yeah, I was in this room, um, there were a bunch of secretaries, and you
Had to, like, give them your papers or something, and then pass go, and then they would, like,
Place you in the next life or something like that.
So I was, like, waiting there, and there were, like, you know, all kinds of different people
There, like, fucked up people and happy people or whatever, and I was, like, one of the,
Like, healthy people.
So I passed through, and then I, like, floated up and floated through, like, the light or
Something, and then was, like, implanted in my mother's womb type of a thing, and then
Born.
Like, that was kind of a vision that I had.
And another weird vision I had, I was laying in bed once, and my, uh, I think I could have
Sworn I was awake.
Like, and, like, all of these, like, cartoon-colored monsters.
Like, like a cartoon.
Fuzzy things.
I mean, they, they weren't scary-looking, but they popped up out of all sides of my bed,
And the way I got, they go, like, right above me, and I freaked out, I screamed, and I ran
Upstairs, and I go, monsters, I saw monsters, and my parents were, like, what?
No, you didn't.
I was, like, I'm sleeping with you tonight.
Yeah, and once I remember, I put my finger in the socket, and I got electrocuted, and
I felt like my entire finger was inside the socket.
And then I looked at the socket, and I was, like, well, how is my finger inside the socket
If it's not big enough to fit my finger?
And I was, like, convinced that my finger was inside the socket, because I could feel
All of the electricity.
That was trippy.
I remember the first time, I remember when I was a little kid, I'd pee sitting down on
The toilet, but I remember the very first time I saw my dad peeing, standing up, so
That's when I started peeing, standing up.
And ever since then, I peed standing up.
And I remember he was always peeing.
He must have drunk in a lot of water, because he was always peeing.
And he did, he had some other habits, like, he would sleep with his head underneath the
Pillow, which I thought was weird.
But then my parents would let me sleep with them a lot.
And they would actually, my dad mainly, had the habit of sleeping with me until I went
To bed.
He'd, like, hold my hand.
And I'd want him to do that.
And as I got older, he didn't want to do it, but I'd, like, talk him and do it, and
Then he'd do it.
And he kept doing that pretty late.
And, yeah, I did, I remember doing yoga on the northern side.
I think my mom was a yoga instructor back, like, before I was born.
On the northern side of the pool that we had, we'd do yoga.
And we'd do, like, the tree.
I remember doing the tree.
And I could swim all the way across the pool from the very beginning.
I remember.
I thought that was really cool.
And I remember once my mom put my brother in the pool, and he was upside down.
She, like, let go of him once, and he was swimming upside down.
And I remember seeing this, like, look of terror in his eyes.
He was a baby.
He was, like, a year old or less than a year old.
And my mom was, like, oh, whoops, and picked him up.
And he was, like, swimming.
And he was, like, ah, like, crying, you know.
We had this submarine, this toy submarine, too.
That was cool.
And then it was, like, I remember we had these, like, toy planes.
And I remember flying the planes underwater because they could fly because the water was thicker.
And that was really cool.
And, yeah, I remember the neighbors just to ourself.
They had some kids.
And there was this girl who lived there because I remember going in her bedroom.
I didn't actually see her.
I didn't talk to her, but maybe I saw her, like, once.
But she had this, like, bed that had, like, the curtains that would cover the bed, like a princess bed.
And I thought that was kind of interesting.
And, yeah, I remember some parties we had, birthday parties.
Like, the kids were playing around on the, like, octagon, like, toy thing.
And we had, like, this, like, sand pit that we'd play into with our toys.
And, yeah, so, anyways, then we moved to Boulder one summer.
And I'm driving up.
And we lived in this house on 6th Street for six months.
And there was this woman who was, like, our roommate.
She lived with us in that house for a while.
And I remember she was six feet tall.
That's just something I remember about her.
She had short, like, hair.
And there was this, like, little creek behind the house.
It was, like, you know, near the Flatirons.
And there was this little kid's playpen there.
And the woman would tell us that there were these munchkin people, like, three-foot-tall people who lived there.
And I believed her.
And I was like, where are they?
I want to see them.
And, yeah, so we played around there.
And then we moved into our house, which is on Mariposa Street.
It was a really big house.
So I had my own room next to my brother's room.
And then we started school at Vidya.
It was the Buddhist school.
And, yeah, I remember my first day.
I was, like, the new kid.
And we went off on, like, a hike.
We bought a show place with all the kids from Vidya.
And the first kid I talked to, and I think we were talking because we, like, had the same kind of sandwich or something, was Jonathan McKeever.
And so then he became my friend.
And it turned out later that he was actually the leader of the class because, I don't know, because he was just the nicest guy and laid-back dude.
And he was the tallest kid.
And he was, like, and so, like, whenever we'd go to, on, like, every day we would, we would, Mr. Brown would put us in his brown or his green, like, Ford van.
Stuff us in there.
And he was a nice guy with his mustache.
And I think he lived on Mariposa Street a couple blocks up from us.
And, like, Jonathan McKeever would sing, like, chants.
Like, we are going to the park.
And then we'd all sing the song, we'd all go into the park.
But I think it was just the guys who'd do that, the girls, kind of, maybe.
I don't know if the girls would chant that, too.
But when we were all, like, roaming around, it was the girls in their group and the guys in our group.
And I remember once at one park we went to, there was this new girl.
I don't remember what grade this was in, but there was this new girl.
And she was supposed to be, like, a really fast runner.
She was, like, a running racer.
But, like, I was with the guys and we were, like, looking at these, like, tadpole things, water skippers.
These things that could, these, like, insects could walk on top of water.
And we thought that was really cool.
But, like, the girls decided that it would be funny if they would, like, pour, like, fill up cups with, like, that pond water and splash us with it.
And, like, that girl.
And so then, like, the guys would, like, run after the girls and try and splash the girls.
But, like, that girl, like, splashed me with the water.
So I picked up a cup.
And I ran after her.
And I remember the girls going, run like a horse.
Or whatever her name was.
Run like a horse.
And, like, I was running after her.
And then, I guess, I don't know why she did this.
But maybe she thought she had gotten away from me.
But she stopped and she turned around and faced me.
And so I splashed her.
But I guess I got some of it in her mouth.
And it totally freaked her out.
And she started, like, crying and ran after the girls and, like, hysterical.
And so then all the girls, like, decided they hated me that day.
And so they disappeared.
And so when it was time to go back to the school, like, they couldn't find the girls.
So we had to, like, get it all in the van and, like, drive all over and find them.
And then we found them, like, hiding under this, like, in somebody's front yard, like, under something.
And they were trying to run away from us.
And we had to grab them.
But that was, I mean, they didn't stay angry with me.
That was just that episode.
